name: Rhubarb Upside Down Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Grease the sides of a 9-inch springform pan; pour in butter and sprinkle with sugar.
2. Cut rhubarb into 2-inch pieces.
3. Arrange the rhubarb in tight rows in the bottom of the pan, starting with the middle three rows and trimming to fit. Chop any remaining pieces coarsely and sprinkle on top with orange rind.
4. In a large bowl, cream together butter and sugar until fluffy. Beat in eggs one at a time and vanilla.
5. Stir together flour, baking powder, baking soda, orange rind, and salt.
6. Stir half of the flour mixture into the butter mixture; stir in yogurt and the remaining flour mixture.
7. Spread batter carefully over rhubarb, mounding higher at the edges.
8. Wrap foil around the bottom of the pan and place on a baking sheet.
9. Bake in a 350°F oven for about 1 hour, or until the cake springs back when lightly touched.
10. Let cool on a rack for 15 minutes. Invert the cake onto a serving platter and serve warm.
